title: Canada Data Residency Baseline (PIPEDA + provincial)
notes: Constrains placement to Canadian cloud regions. PIPEDA uses an organization-to-organization accountability model (Principle 4.1.3) rather than an adequacy model, and provincial regimes (BC FOIPPA, Quebec Law 25) require a privacy impact assessment before storing or disclosing personal information outside the province/country. Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not legal advice.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to a Canadian region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed Canadian cloud region (Montreal/Toronto/Calgary/Quebec) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: Keeping workloads in Canada avoids a cross-border transfer for which PIPEDA Principle 4.1.3 keeps the organization accountable and which Quebec Law 25 subjects to a mandatory pre-transfer assessment.

articles.title: Assessment before disclosing personal information outside Quebec

rules.name: No unrestricted or cross-border egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress or external-name proxying can transfer personal information outside Canada, keeping the organization accountable under PIPEDA Principle 4.1.3 without a comparable-protection safeguard.

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the organization controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys are a security safeguard proportional to the sensitivity of the information under PIPEDA Principle 4.7.

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is a baseline safeguard against loss, theft and unauthorized access under PIPEDA Principle 4.7.
